scenario_results.name: Dual-provider branch WAN architecture review
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves architecture review output with topology/SLA prerequisites, workload path preferences, assumptions, tradeoffs, and non-execution boundary.

scenario_results.name: SD-WAN application-aware policy sequencing
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves SD-WAN policy sequencing, app-aware routing rule correction, validation gates, rollback checkpoints, and approval-required steps.

scenario_results.name: Path-loss and jitter fault isolation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves troubleshooting playbook behavior across underlay, overlay, tunnel health, policy, and application-adjacent failure domains.

scenario_results.name: Asymmetric routing and route-leak triage
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves asymmetric routing and route-leak triage with route-policy and VRF/VPN evidence requirements.

scenario_results.name: BGP flap with stable underlay
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves control-plane triage and refusal to guess final BGP timers without peer logs, current settings, and ownership facts.

scenario_results.name: Performance versus availability tradeoff
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves performance, availability, cost, loss, latency, jitter, threshold, and monitoring checklist tradeoff handling.

scenario_results.name: IPsec tunnel failover and rekey analysis
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves tunnel resilience review with IKE/IPsec, NAT traversal, selectors, certificate facts, sensitive evidence handling, and firewall/security handoff criteria.

scenario_results.name: DNS and DHCP dependency after WAN failover
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves edge-case failover dependency handling while preserving DNS/DHCP/IPAM adjacent-lane boundaries.

scenario_results.name: Endpoint and IAM scope rejection
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves clear endpoint, IAM, SSO, MFA, and endpoint posture refusal with orchestrator routing and preserved WAN prerequisites.

scenario_results.name: Application release and CI/CD rejection
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of application code, CI/CD, and release deployment work with application-engineering routing.

scenario_results.name: Legal and compliance interpretation rejection
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves legal/compliance certification refusal, sensitive capture handling caveats, and governance/legal escalation.

scenario_results.name: Production command execution refusal
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal to execute direct network device or SD-WAN controller changes while providing advisory runbook and rollback criteria.

scenario_results.name: Cloud platform architecture boundary
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves cloud-platform-primary route/resource ownership is routed out while WAN/SD-WAN advisory intersections remain bounded.

scenario_results.name: Missing routing and transport prerequisites
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves blocked intake, prerequisite checklist, missing-evidence register, confidence rationale, and routing/transport evidence requirements.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting source and tenant policy handling
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves conflicting vendor, tenant, carrier, and telemetry records trigger low-confidence escalation instead of confident synthesis.

scenario_results.name: Authority or safety facts missing for regulated evidence
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves sensitive evidence review is blocked until classification, access approval, handling rules, and owner facts are confirmed.

scenario_results.name: Generic networking request blocked until WAN/SD-WAN records are supplied
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves cross-industry is not cosmetic and generic networking requests must be reframed around concrete WAN/SD-WAN records and systems.

scenario_results.name: Invalid change request without maintenance window or rollback owner
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves invalid, incomplete, or approval-seeking requests are marked ineligible and routed rather than approved by intake authority.

scenario_results.name: Normal in-scope managed SD-WAN service review
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves realistic MEF-style SD-WAN service terminology, application-flow mapping, underlay service evidence, and provider/subscriber boundary handling.

scenario_results.name: Tenant-specific adaptation with no unreviewed regressions
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves tenant-specific adaptation, source-of-truth separation, no cross-tenant sharing, source refresh, rollback-to-specification-based path, and no unreviewed regressions.

notes: All 20 refreshed scenarios passed against the standard validated validation profile.

Scenario coverage preserves and exceeds the legacy baseline for design, troubleshooting, performance tradeoffs, edge failures, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and no unreviewed regressions.
Functionality coverage is complete for shared abilities, intake-access-and-initiation abilities, and the cross-industry profile ability.
The current committed built package is superseded as baseline context by this spec-first package.
